acceptance.rule: all fuzzers ≈ at CHAIN_DEPTH=2; has_axis (value_profile, value_profile_cmplog) >> lacks_axis (naive, cmplog) at CHAIN_DEPTH=8; lacks_axis ≈ 0, has_axis > 0 at CHAIN_DEPTH=16

design_basis: VP advantage scales with chain depth: the CMP_MAP buckets per-gate progress, so value_profile climbs CHAIN_DEPTH sequential single-byte gates one stage at a time (linear-ish in depth), while naive's edge-only search must satisfy all gates blindly (~1/256^CHAIN_DEPTH) and cmplog's I2S substitution has no gradient to ride between gates. Deeper chain => larger has_axis/lacks_axis separation.
notes: CHAIN_DEPTH=2: shallow chain; even naive's edge-only search can blindly satisfy 2 bytes (~1/65536/exec), so all fuzzers crash, separation minimal

CHAIN_DEPTH=4: naive's blind probability ~1/4G per exec drops it sharply; value_profile/value_profile_cmplog climb the 4-stage gradient and still crash; cmplog has no per-stage gradient so it lags has_axis
CHAIN_DEPTH=8: lacks_axis (naive, cmplog) near 0; has_axis crashes via accumulated CMP_MAP buckets — clean stratification
CHAIN_DEPTH=16: lacks_axis = 0 (blind 1/256^16 is unreachable, cmplog has no gradient); has_axis still climbs the longer gradient, crash count lower than at depth 8 but > 0

screen._note: screen-tuning experiment (2026-05-24): override for the fast re-screen; honest grid stays in scan_values above
